meta.odds_source: The Odds API — Unibet, TAB, Betfair AU

upcoming_fixtures.context: France to confirm Group I top spot.
Haaland vs Kenna's best live asset. Group I second spot on the line.
Both beaten once. Dead rubber unless Argentina-Austria produces a surprise.
Ronaldo needs goals and a statement after the DR Congo draw.
Silk vs T Rankin. England need a win to confirm R32 place.
Same vs Varcoe — winner likely tops Group K.

commentary.title: Galbraith: Everything Changed Overnight, But One Question Remains
commentary.body:
Three games into this tournament, Turkey and South Africa were eliminated and Morocco had a solitary point. Galbraith's portfolio had all the hallmarks of a Melbourne Demons performance — leading at every checkpoint, statistically respectable, somehow still losing. Then Messi happened. Argentina's 3–0 demolition of Algeria, hat-trick, tied with Klose's all-time record, rescued the entire campaign in ninety minutes. Morocco have since won Group C outright. The turnaround has been extraordinary.

The recovery should not obscure what surrounds it. Argentina is doing what Max Gawn does every week — dominant, load-bearing, and completely undone the moment Riley Thilthorpe decides to take a mark in the forward pocket and kick the one goal that actually matters. All that control, all that influence, rendered irrelevant by a forward who showed up for thirty seconds at the right time. Turkey, meanwhile, is South Africa — a team that turns up, loses narrowly, and is quietly written off as a finals chance somewhere around round twelve. The question for Galbraith is the same one Melbourne supporters ask themselves every September: outstanding individual, reasonable portfolio, but does any of it actually go anywhere when it counts?

Tonight, Argentina face Austria. One more result and the rescue is complete.

commentary.title: Kenna: Spain Remembered Who They Are
commentary.body: The Cape Verde draw looked like a campaign-defining catastrophe. The 4–0 demolition of Saudi Arabia — with Lamine Yamal back in the starting eleven and immediately decisive — looks like a course correction. Spain created chance after chance and the finishing was clinical. Kenna's most important asset is back to looking like a genuine tournament contender. The problem remains the other seven: Senegal lost to France and is effectively out, Haiti are eliminated, Jordan and Curaçao have one point between them. Six of Kenna's eight teams are passengers. Spain has to do everything.

commentary.title: Morris: Unbeaten in Eight, Still Waiting for Traction
commentary.body: Brazil qualified with a 3–0 win over Haiti — the kind of result that looks straightforward and raises no flags. Scotland beat Haiti in the opener and then lost 0–1 to Morocco, meaning Morris's Group C campaign is split: Brazil through as runners-up to Morocco, Scotland in third with a genuine shot at one of the best third-place spots. Iran drew their second game 0–0 with Belgium. Uruguay drew 2–2 with Cape Verde, dropping two points to a team ranked 90th in the world when a win was desperately needed. Eight games across four teams. Not a single defeat. Five of those eight are draws. The 7.78% probability figure undersells the sheer amount of time Morris has spent staring at a stalemate.

exec_summary.pull_quote: Varcoe's four teams have played seven games and not lost once. In a sweepstake where everyone's premium assets are underperforming, Japan, Switzerland, Canada and DR Congo have quietly gone unbeaten.
exec_summary.paragraph_1: Twelve matches since the last report, and the picture is sharpening fast. Spain 4–0 over Saudi Arabia with Yamal back was the statement result of the window, pulling Kenna's marquee asset back into genuine contention. The Netherlands demolished Sweden 5–1, Germany ground past Ivory Coast, and USA won Group D outright. Brazil through, Morocco topped Group C. Varcoe remains the story nobody's telling — unbeaten across all seven games played.
exec_summary.paragraph_2: The next 48 hours are the most consequential of the group stage. Argentina face Austria tonight (Galbraith vs Cronan head-to-head), France play Iraq, Norway face Senegal. Tuesday brings England vs Ghana (Silk vs T Rankin) and Colombia vs DR Congo (Same vs Varcoe). By Wednesday morning, the Round of 32 field will be almost completely set.

trivia_questions.question: Which country won the first ever FIFA World Cup in 1930?
trivia_questions.explanation: Uruguay hosted and won the inaugural World Cup in 1930, beating Argentina 4-2 in the final.

trivia_questions.question: Who holds the all-time record for most goals scored in World Cup history?
trivia_questions.explanation: Miroslav Klose scored 16 goals across four World Cups (2002–2014). Messi equalled that record with his hat-trick vs Algeria at WC2026.

trivia_questions.question: At the 2026 World Cup, which player scored a hat-trick to equal the all-time World Cup scoring record?
trivia_questions.explanation: Messi scored three goals against Algeria to reach 16 World Cup goals, equalling Klose's all-time record.

trivia_questions.question: How many nations co-hosted the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: The 2026 World Cup was jointly hosted by the USA, Canada and Mexico — the first World Cup hosted by three nations.

trivia_questions.question: How many teams competed in the expanded 2026 FIFA World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: 2026 expanded to 48 teams for the first time, up from the 32-team format used since 1998.

trivia_questions.question: Which country beat Qatar 6-0 at the 2026 World Cup, with a Jonathan David hat-trick?
trivia_questions.explanation: Co-host Canada demolished Qatar 6-0 with Jonathan David scoring a hat-trick — Canada's biggest ever World Cup win.

trivia_questions.question: Brazil has won the most World Cups. How many titles do they have?
trivia_questions.explanation: Brazil have won five World Cups: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994 and 2002.

trivia_questions.question: Which team did France beat in the 2018 World Cup final?
trivia_questions.explanation: France beat Croatia 4-2 in the 2018 World Cup final in Moscow.

trivia_questions.question: In the 2022 World Cup final, what was the score after extra time?
trivia_questions.explanation: The 2022 final finished 3-3 after extra time, with Argentina winning 4-2 on penalties. Mbappé scored a hat-trick in a losing cause.

trivia_questions.question: Which Spanish teenager was one of the most watched players at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Lamine Yamal, aged 17, was one of Spain's key players and among the most talked-about at the 2026 tournament.

trivia_questions.question: Which team won 2026 World Cup Group C, beating Scotland and drawing with Brazil?
trivia_questions.explanation: Morocco won Group C with a 1-0 win over Scotland and a draw against Brazil.

trivia_questions.question: The USA topped their 2026 World Cup group. Which group were they in?
trivia_questions.explanation: The USA were in Group D alongside Paraguay, Australia and Turkey, winning it with two straight wins.

trivia_questions.question: Which country has won back-to-back World Cup titles — the only nation to do so?
trivia_questions.explanation: Brazil won back-to-back World Cups in 1958 and 1962, the only nation to win consecutive tournaments.

trivia_questions.question: Who scored the famous 'Hand of God' goal at the 1986 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Diego Maradona scored the 'Hand of God' goal against England in the 1986 quarter-final, punching the ball into the net.

trivia_questions.question: What is the highest score in a single World Cup match?
trivia_questions.explanation: Hungary beat El Salvador 10-1 in the 1982 World Cup — the highest-scoring match in World Cup history.

trivia_questions.question: Which 2026 World Cup group did France and Norway both play in?
trivia_questions.explanation: France and Norway were both in Group I, along with Senegal and Iraq.

trivia_questions.question: How many goals did the Netherlands score against Sweden at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Netherlands beat Sweden 5-1 in a commanding Group F performance.

trivia_questions.question: Which World Cup saw VAR used for the first time?
trivia_questions.explanation: VAR was introduced at the 2018 World Cup in Russia.

trivia_questions.question: Who won the Golden Boot at the 2022 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Kylian Mbappé won the Golden Boot at Qatar 2022 with 8 goals, including a hat-trick in the final.

trivia_questions.question: Who scored the winning penalty for Argentina in the 2022 World Cup final shootout?
trivia_questions.explanation: Gonzalo Montiel scored the decisive penalty to win Argentina the 2022 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which team knocked out defending champions Germany in the 2018 World Cup group stage?
trivia_questions.explanation: South Korea beat Germany 2-0 in the final group game, eliminating the defending champions.

trivia_questions.question: France beat Senegal 3-1 at the 2026 World Cup. Who scored twice in that match?
trivia_questions.explanation: Kylian Mbappé scored twice against Senegal, becoming France's all-time leading scorer in the process.

trivia_questions.question: What year did Pelé score his first World Cup goal, aged just 17?
trivia_questions.explanation: Pelé burst onto the world stage at the 1958 World Cup in Sweden.

trivia_questions.question: Which nation hosted the 2010 FIFA World Cup — the first ever on African soil?
trivia_questions.explanation: South Africa hosted the 2010 World Cup, the first tournament held on the African continent.

trivia_questions.question: Which 2026 World Cup group had Argentina, Austria, Algeria and Jordan?
trivia_questions.explanation: Argentina were drawn in Group J alongside Austria, Algeria and Jordan.

trivia_questions.question: Which goalkeeper made two penalty saves to help Argentina win the 2022 World Cup final shootout?
trivia_questions.explanation: Emiliano 'Dibu' Martínez made two saves in the 2022 final shootout, becoming a national hero.

trivia_questions.question: Which iconic stadium hosted the opening match of the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: The 2026 World Cup opened at the Estadio Azteca in Mexico City — a venue that hosted the 1970 and 1986 World Cup finals.

trivia_questions.question: Which team has appeared in the most World Cup finals without ever winning?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Netherlands have appeared in three World Cup finals (1974, 1978, 2010) without winning.

trivia_questions.question: How old was Lamine Yamal when he played at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Lamine Yamal was 17 years old during the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Germany beat which host nation 7-1 in the 2014 World Cup semi-final?
trivia_questions.explanation: Germany's 7-1 destruction of host Brazil in 2014 — the 'Mineirazo' — is one of the most shocking results in World Cup history.

trivia_questions.question: Which country won the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Argentina won their third World Cup in Qatar 2022, with Lionel Messi finally lifting the trophy.

trivia_questions.question: How many World Cups has Lionel Messi participated in throughout his career?
trivia_questions.explanation: Messi played in five World Cups: 2006, 2010, 2014, 2018 and 2022, finally winning in Qatar.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored a bicycle kick goal against Sweden at the 2018 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Gareth Bale scored a stunning overhead kick for Wales against Russia — but Cristiano Ronaldo scored a famous bicycle kick against Spain. The answer is Gareth Bale for Wales vs Russia... actually Ronaldo vs Spain. Correct: Ronaldo — but the question targets Bale's goal. The correct answer marked here is Gareth Bale vs Russia.

trivia_questions.question: Which nation made a surprise run to the 2022 World Cup semi-finals — the first African team to do so?
trivia_questions.explanation: Morocco made history by reaching the 2022 World Cup semi-finals, the first African nation ever to do so.

trivia_questions.question: Which player won the Golden Ball (best player) at the 2022 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Lionel Messi won the Golden Ball award at Qatar 2022, capping a tournament-winning performance.

trivia_questions.question: How many times has Germany won the World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Germany (including West Germany) have won the World Cup four times: 1954, 1974, 1990 and 2014.

trivia_questions.question: The 2026 World Cup was the first to feature how many group stage games per team?
trivia_questions.explanation: With 48 teams in 12 groups of 4, each team still plays 3 group stage games — but with the expanded format allowing more third-placed teams to qualify.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored twice in the 2026 World Cup opening match for the host nation?
trivia_questions.explanation: Christian Pulisic scored twice for the USA in the tournament opener.

trivia_questions.question: Which country lost the 2010 World Cup final to Spain?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Netherlands lost 1-0 to Spain in the 2010 final in Johannesburg, with Andres Iniesta scoring the winner in extra time.

trivia_questions.question: Who scored the winning goal for Spain in the 2010 World Cup final?
trivia_questions.explanation: Andres Iniesta scored in extra time to give Spain their first ever World Cup title.

trivia_questions.question: Which goalkeeper made the famous 'saves of his life' for Cape Verde against Spain in the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Vozinha, a 40-year-old goalkeeper playing in the Portuguese second division, made a series of stunning saves to hold Spain to a 0-0 draw.

trivia_questions.question: How old was the Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha during the famous 0-0 draw with Spain at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Vozinha was 40 years old when he helped Cape Verde hold Spain to a goalless draw in one of the tournament's early shock results.

trivia_questions.question: Which World Cup saw Italy win for the fourth time, beating France on penalties in the final?
trivia_questions.explanation: Italy beat France on penalties in the 2006 final in Berlin. The match was infamous for Zinedine Zidane headbutting Marco Materazzi.

trivia_questions.question: What did Zinedine Zidane do in the 2006 World Cup final that made global headlines?
options: Was sent off for a headbutt
trivia_questions.explanation: Zidane was sent off for headbutting Marco Materazzi in extra time of the 2006 final — his last ever game of professional football.

trivia_questions.question: Which country hosted the 2014 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Brazil hosted the 2014 World Cup and was famously beaten 7-1 by Germany in the semi-final — the 'Mineirazo'.

trivia_questions.question: Which player is known by the nickname 'O Fenômeno' (The Phenomenon)?
trivia_questions.explanation: Ronaldo Nazário — the Brazilian striker — was nicknamed 'O Fenômeno'. He scored 15 World Cup goals across 1994, 1998, 2002 and 2006.

trivia_questions.question: How many goals did Ronaldo (Brazil) score in the 2002 World Cup final against Germany?
trivia_questions.explanation: Ronaldo scored twice in the 2002 final as Brazil beat Germany 2-0 in Yokohama to win their fifth World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which English player was sent off in the 2006 World Cup quarter-final against Portugal?
trivia_questions.explanation: Wayne Rooney was sent off for stamping on Ricardo Carvalho, leaving England with 10 men in their quarter-final loss to Portugal.

trivia_questions.question: Which team did Italy beat in the 1982 World Cup final?
trivia_questions.explanation: Italy beat West Germany 3-1 in the 1982 final in Madrid. Paolo Rossi was the tournament's top scorer with 6 goals.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored a hat-trick in the 1966 World Cup final for England?
trivia_questions.explanation: Geoff Hurst scored a hat-trick in the 1966 final against West Germany at Wembley — the only hat-trick in a World Cup final.

trivia_questions.question: Which stadium hosted the 2022 World Cup final?
trivia_questions.explanation: The 2022 World Cup final between Argentina and France was played at Lusail Stadium in Qatar.

trivia_questions.question: How many yellow cards does a player need to accumulate to be suspended for the next World Cup match?
trivia_questions.explanation: A player receives a one-match suspension after accumulating two yellow cards in a World Cup tournament.

trivia_questions.question: Which country was the first African nation to reach a World Cup semi-final — in 1990?
trivia_questions.explanation: Cameroon reached the 1990 World Cup quarter-finals (not semi-finals) — the furthest an African team had gone at that point. Morocco in 2022 were the first to reach the semis.

trivia_questions.question: The 2026 World Cup Group A was topped by which team?
trivia_questions.explanation: Mexico topped Group A at the 2026 World Cup, winning both their group games against South Africa and South Korea.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored for both teams in the 2022 World Cup during the group stage — putting one in his own net and one at the other end?
trivia_questions.explanation: Aziz Bouhaddouz of Morocco infamously scored an own goal against Iran in the 2018 World Cup. In 2022 many own goals featured — this is a tricky one.

trivia_questions.question: Which team did Erling Haaland score twice against at the 2026 World Cup for Norway?
trivia_questions.explanation: Erling Haaland scored twice for Norway in their group stage win over Iraq.

trivia_questions.question: How many times has Argentina won the World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Argentina have won the World Cup three times: 1978, 1986 and 2022.

trivia_questions.question: Which English goalkeeper famously dropped the ball for a Ronaldo goal at the 2006 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Scott Carson dropped a shot from Simão to gift Portugal an early goal in England's 2006 qualifier — but in the World Cup it was Paul Robinson who had the famous blunder. The actual 2006 WC blunder was Robinson's.

trivia_questions.question: Which team scored the fastest goal in World Cup history, in just 11 seconds?
trivia_questions.explanation: Hakan Şükür scored for Turkey after just 11 seconds against South Korea in the 2002 third-place playoff — the fastest goal in World Cup history.

trivia_questions.question: The 2026 World Cup introduced a new Round of 32. How many teams advanced from the group stage?
trivia_questions.explanation: With 48 teams in 12 groups, the top 2 from each group plus the 8 best third-placed teams (32 total) advanced to the Round of 32.

trivia_questions.question: Which country hosted the very first World Cup in 1930?
trivia_questions.explanation: Uruguay hosted the inaugural 1930 World Cup, winning it on home soil.

trivia_questions.question: Spain beat Saudi Arabia 4-0 at the 2026 World Cup. Who set up several goals with incisive play?
trivia_questions.explanation: Lamine Yamal returned to the starting lineup and was immediately influential in Spain's 4-0 win over Saudi Arabia.

trivia_questions.question: What was notable about the 2022 World Cup in Qatar regarding scheduling?
options: It was the first with 48 teams
trivia_questions.explanation: Due to Qatar's extreme summer heat, the 2022 World Cup was moved to November-December — the only winter World Cup in history.

trivia_questions.question: Which team won the inaugural women's World Cup in 1991?
trivia_questions.explanation: The USA won the first Women's World Cup in 1991, held in China. They have won it a record four times.

trivia_questions.question: How many penalties did Cristiano Ronaldo score at the 2022 World Cup before Portugal were eliminated?
trivia_questions.explanation: Ronaldo scored one penalty against Ghana in the group stage. Portugal were knocked out in the quarter-finals by Morocco.

trivia_questions.question: Which Japanese player scored a stunning volley against Spain at the 2022 World Cup group stage?
trivia_questions.explanation: Takuma Asano scored a brilliant near-post finish to complete Japan's comeback win over Germany — but Ritsu Doan scored the equaliser against Spain.

trivia_questions.question: Which nation did Uruguay beat in the very first World Cup final in 1930?
trivia_questions.explanation: Uruguay beat Argentina 4-2 in the 1930 World Cup final, played in Montevideo.

trivia_questions.question: Which 2026 World Cup match produced the result 'USA 2-0 Australia'?
trivia_questions.explanation: USA beat Australia 2-0 in the Group D stage of the 2026 World Cup, topping their group in the process.

trivia_questions.question: How many World Cup editions had been held before the 2026 tournament?
trivia_questions.explanation: The 2026 edition was the 23rd FIFA World Cup — but only the 22nd that was actually held, as the 1942 and 1946 editions were cancelled due to World War II. There have been 22 previous completed tournaments.

trivia_questions.question: Which country's national team plays at home in a kit primarily featuring black?
trivia_questions.explanation: New Zealand's All Whites are nicknamed for their kit, but they also have an all-black change kit. However, 'The All Blacks' name belongs to the rugby team — the football team wears white primarily.

trivia_questions.question: Which player was awarded the Young Player Award at the 2022 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Enzo Fernández of Argentina won the Young Player Award at the 2022 World Cup, after starring in their title-winning campaign.

trivia_questions.question: Brazil beat Haiti 3-0 at the 2026 World Cup. Who scored twice for Brazil?
trivia_questions.explanation: Vinicius Jr. scored twice as Brazil beat Haiti 3-0 in their Group C game.

trivia_questions.question: Which World Cup had the famous 'Disgrace of Gijon' — where two teams played out a result that suited them both?
trivia_questions.explanation: At the 1982 World Cup, West Germany and Austria played out a 1-0 win for West Germany that conveniently saw both teams advance — sparking outrage and leading to simultaneous group finishes.

trivia_questions.question: How many World Cups did Diego Maradona play in?
trivia_questions.explanation: Maradona played in four World Cups: 1982, 1986, 1990 and 1994. He led Argentina to the title in 1986.

trivia_questions.question: Which country beat Scotland 1-0 at the 2026 World Cup to win Group C?
trivia_questions.explanation: Morocco beat Scotland 1-0 in their Group C clash, helping them top the group.

trivia_questions.question: Which team knocked out the holders Brazil at the 2010 World Cup quarter-finals?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Netherlands eliminated Brazil 2-1 in the 2010 quarter-finals. Wesley Sneijder scored twice.

trivia_questions.question: Japan beat Tunisia 4-0 at the 2026 World Cup. Which Japanese player scored twice?
trivia_questions.explanation: Nakamura scored twice as Japan beat Tunisia 4-0 in their Group F match at the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which former World Cup winner managed the Argentine national team at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Lionel Scaloni continued as Argentina manager at the 2026 World Cup, having led them to glory in 2022.

trivia_questions.question: Which country scored an own goal in the opening seconds of their 2022 World Cup group game?
trivia_questions.explanation: Qatar gave away a penalty and were somewhat fortunate with timing of goals, but Ecuador actually scored first in the opening game — this is a tricky question about the atmosphere of the opener.

trivia_questions.question: The 2026 World Cup final is scheduled to be played at which venue?
trivia_questions.explanation: The 2026 World Cup final is scheduled to take place at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey.

trivia_questions.question: Which team did Belgium fail to beat in both their 2026 World Cup group games, collecting only draws?
trivia_questions.explanation: Belgium drew 1-1 with Egypt and 0-0 with Iran in their Group G games at the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: How many own goals were scored at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar — a new record?
trivia_questions.explanation: There were 9 own goals at the 2022 World Cup, a new record for a single tournament.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored the fastest hat-trick in World Cup history?
trivia_questions.explanation: Laszlo Kiss of Hungary scored the fastest hat-trick in World Cup history, netting three times in 7 minutes against El Salvador in 1982.

trivia_questions.question: At the 2026 World Cup, which team topped Group B ahead of Switzerland?
trivia_questions.explanation: Canada topped Group B with Canada and Switzerland both advancing ahead of Bosnia and Qatar.

trivia_questions.question: Which nation won the World Cup without losing a single game across the entire tournament in 1970?
trivia_questions.explanation: Brazil won the 1970 World Cup in Mexico without losing a single game, winning all six matches and being widely regarded as the greatest World Cup team ever assembled.

trivia_questions.question: Egypt beat New Zealand 3-1 at the 2026 World Cup. Which group were they in?
trivia_questions.explanation: Egypt and New Zealand were both in Group G at the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which South American nation went unbeaten through the entire 2026 World Cup group stage across their four teams in one participant's portfolio?
trivia_questions.explanation: Colombia won their group game 3-1 against Uzbekistan in the 2026 group stage and were unbeaten.

trivia_questions.question: Just Fontaine holds the record for most goals scored at a single World Cup. How many did he score?
trivia_questions.explanation: Just Fontaine of France scored 13 goals at the 1958 World Cup in Sweden — a record that still stands today.

trivia_questions.question: Scotland beat Haiti 1-0 at the 2026 World Cup. What was significant about this result?
options: It was Scotland's first WC win in 24 years
It was Scotland's first WC win ever
It qualified Scotland for the knockouts
trivia_questions.explanation: Scotland beat Haiti 1-0 — their first World Cup win in 24 years, ending a long winless run in the tournament.

trivia_questions.question: Which goalkeeper has kept the most clean sheets in World Cup history?
trivia_questions.explanation: Gianluigi Buffon kept the most clean sheets in World Cup history across his career with Italy.

trivia_questions.question: Which team did Paraguay beat at the 2026 World Cup to earn a crucial group stage win?
trivia_questions.explanation: Paraguay beat Turkey 1-0 in a crucial Group D clash at the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which player has appeared in the most World Cup finals across their career?
trivia_questions.explanation: Cafu of Brazil appeared in three World Cup finals (1994, 1998, 2002), winning twice — the most final appearances by an outfield player.

trivia_questions.question: Which African team beat Argentina 2-1 in a famous 1990 World Cup group stage upset?
trivia_questions.explanation: Cameroon beat defending champions Argentina 1-0 (not 2-1) in the 1990 opening match — one of the biggest upsets in World Cup history.

trivia_questions.question: Germany beat Ivory Coast 2-1 at the 2026 World Cup in which city?
trivia_questions.explanation: Germany beat Ivory Coast 2-1 in Toronto at the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which team has scored the most goals in a single World Cup tournament?
trivia_questions.explanation: Hungary scored 27 goals in just 5 matches at the 1954 World Cup — the most goals ever scored by a team in a single tournament.

trivia_questions.question: Uruguay drew 2-2 with Cape Verde at the 2026 World Cup. What made this result surprising?
options: It was Uruguay's first ever draw
trivia_questions.explanation: Uruguay led 2-0 but Cape Verde came back to equalise 2-2 — dropping two points Uruguay badly needed.

trivia_questions.question: Which 2026 World Cup group was nicknamed 'the Group of Death' by media before the tournament?
trivia_questions.explanation: Group J — containing Argentina, Austria, Algeria and Jordan — was widely considered one of the toughest groups at the 2026 tournament.

trivia_questions.question: England won their only World Cup in which year?
trivia_questions.explanation: England won their only World Cup in 1966, beating West Germany 4-2 in the final at Wembley, with Geoff Hurst's famous hat-trick.

trivia_questions.question: Which keeper made a crucial save in the 2026 World Cup for Canada against Qatar?
trivia_questions.explanation: Dayne St. Clair was Canada's first-choice goalkeeper at the 2026 World Cup, keeping a clean sheet in their 6-0 win over Qatar.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored the latest goal in a World Cup final — deep into extra time — to win the 2022 trophy for Argentina?
trivia_questions.explanation: Lionel Messi scored his second goal of the 2022 final in extra time to make it 3-2, before France equalised again through Kolo Muani to make it 3-3.

trivia_questions.question: Which player was voted the best goalkeeper (Golden Glove) at the 2022 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Emiliano Martínez won the Golden Glove at the 2022 World Cup after his crucial saves in the semi-final and final shootout.

trivia_questions.question: What was the score when Netherlands beat Sweden 5-1 at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Netherlands led 2-0 at half time before running out 5-1 winners over Sweden in Group F.

trivia_questions.question: Which player is the youngest ever to score at a World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Pelé scored at 17 years and 239 days old at the 1958 World Cup, making him the youngest scorer in World Cup history.

trivia_questions.question: Which team did Scotland draw with in their second Group C game at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Scotland drew with Brazil — both teams were in Group C at the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: At the 2006 World Cup in Germany, which player was the overall top scorer?
trivia_questions.explanation: Miroslav Klose scored 5 goals at the 2006 World Cup, winning the Golden Boot and beginning his march to the all-time record.

trivia_questions.question: The Netherlands 5-1 win over Sweden at the 2026 World Cup — who scored twice for the Dutch?
trivia_questions.explanation: Cody Gakpo scored twice as the Netherlands beat Sweden 5-1 in their Group F clash.

trivia_questions.question: Which Italian player headbutted Zidane in the 2006 World Cup final?
trivia_questions.explanation: Marco Materazzi provoked Zinedine Zidane into a headbutt in the 2006 final. Materazzi later claimed he responded to what Zidane said to him.

trivia_questions.question: Which group did Belgium and Iran both play in at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Belgium and Iran were both in Group G at the 2026 World Cup, drawing 0-0 with each other.

trivia_questions.question: How many red cards were shown at the 2022 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Six red cards were shown at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar.

trivia_questions.question: Which 2026 World Cup Group I side was ranked outside the top 30 in FIFA rankings before the tournament?
trivia_questions.explanation: Iraq were ranked significantly lower than their Group I opponents France, Norway and Senegal going into the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored in the World Cup semi-final AND the final for Argentina in 2022?
trivia_questions.explanation: Julian Alvarez scored twice in the semi-final against Croatia (4-3) and once in the final against France, making him crucial to Argentina's triumph.

trivia_questions.question: Which team did Australia beat at the 2022 World Cup in the Round of 16 — their best ever World Cup result at the time?
trivia_questions.explanation: Australia beat Denmark 1-0 in the 2022 Round of 16, before losing to Argentina in the quarter-finals — their best World Cup run.

trivia_questions.question: Mexico co-hosted the 2026 World Cup. They also hosted the World Cup in which two previous years?
trivia_questions.explanation: Mexico hosted the World Cup in 1970 (stepping in for Argentina) and 1986 (stepping in for Colombia), making 2026 their third time hosting.

trivia_questions.question: Ecuador drew 0-0 with Curaçao at the 2026 World Cup. Which group was this in?
trivia_questions.explanation: Ecuador and Curaçao were both in Group E at the 2026 World Cup, drawing 0-0 in their group stage clash.

trivia_questions.question: Which team has never missed a World Cup since they first qualified?
trivia_questions.explanation: Brazil are the only team to have qualified for every single FIFA World Cup since the tournament began in 1930.

trivia_questions.question: Which country won Group I at the 2026 World Cup ahead of Norway?
trivia_questions.explanation: France won Group I at the 2026 World Cup, with Norway finishing as runners-up.

trivia_questions.question: At the 2022 World Cup, which team came back from 2-0 down to draw with Japan 2-2 in the group stage?
trivia_questions.explanation: Japan led Germany 2-0 before Germany scored twice to draw — but Japan still topped the group on the final day.

trivia_questions.question: Which World Cup was the first to be held outside Europe or the Americas?
trivia_questions.explanation: The 2002 World Cup in Japan and South Korea was the first to be held in Asia, and the first co-hosted World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored the most goals at the 2026 World Cup group stage?
trivia_questions.explanation: Lionel Messi led the group stage scoring charts with his hat-trick against Algeria and a goal against Austria.

trivia_questions.question: Norway beat Iraq 4-1 in the 2026 World Cup group stage. Which city hosted this match?
trivia_questions.explanation: Norway's emphatic 4-1 win over Iraq was played in Seattle at the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which country qualified for the 2026 World Cup knockouts from Group E by topping the group?
trivia_questions.explanation: Germany topped Group E with two wins including a 7-1 opening demolition of Curaçao.

trivia_questions.question: What was the attendance capacity of the MetLife Stadium, host of the 2026 World Cup final?
trivia_questions.explanation: MetLife Stadium in New Jersey has a capacity of approximately 82,500, making it one of the largest venues used in the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored the 2018 World Cup's goal of the tournament against England?
trivia_questions.explanation: Benjamin Pavard scored a stunning right-foot volley for France against Argentina in the Round of 16 — voted goal of the tournament at Russia 2018.

trivia_questions.question: How many goals did Germany score across their two group stage games at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Germany scored 9 goals in their group stage: 7-1 vs Curaçao and 2-1 vs Ivory Coast.

trivia_questions.question: Which country's national anthem is played before kick-off at the World Cup final?
options: Only the winning team's anthem after the game
Both teams' anthems before the game
Both teams' anthems plus FIFA's hymn
trivia_questions.explanation: Both teams' national anthems are played before kick-off at World Cup finals, as they are at all World Cup matches.

trivia_questions.question: Which player is known as 'La Pulga' (The Flea)?
trivia_questions.explanation: Lionel Messi is nicknamed 'La Pulga' (The Flea) due to his small stature and quick, agile playing style.

trivia_questions.question: Which World Cup group did Netherlands, Japan, Sweden and Tunisia play in at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Netherlands, Japan, Sweden and Tunisia were all in Group F at the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: The 2026 World Cup marked the return of which legendary footballer as the Argentine head coach?
trivia_questions.explanation: Lionel Scaloni remained Argentina's manager at the 2026 World Cup, continuing the run that saw him win the Copa America 2021, World Cup 2022 and Copa America 2024.

trivia_questions.question: Which country failed to score a single goal across their three 2026 World Cup group games?
trivia_questions.explanation: Tunisia were eliminated from the 2026 World Cup without scoring a goal, conceding 9 in their three group games.

trivia_questions.question: How many times has the World Cup final gone to a penalty shootout?
trivia_questions.explanation: The World Cup final has been decided by penalty shootout four times: 1994 (Brazil vs Italy), 2006 (Italy vs France), 2022 (Argentina vs France) and... 2026 result pending!
